summary: Obfuscated bulk email/SMS sending toolkit with offensive-tooling capabilities
details: Package self-describes as an 'Advanced email sender' with keywords including 'red-team' and 'smtp'. All 45 source files under.ad/ are heavily obfuscated using javascript-obfuscator with RC4-encoded string arrays, control-flow flattening, and self-defending wrappers. Decoded contents expose CLI subcommands for bulk SMTP email sending, bulk SMS through Twilio/Bandwidth/SMTP-to-SMS gateways, and OAuth device-code flows that attach victim Microsoft/Google accounts to harvest contacts and sent-items via Graph API endpoints (graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/contacts, /me/people, /me/mailFolders/sentitems/messages) and IMAP, plus open-redirect/phishing helpers. The postinstall script is benign — it exits on non-Windows and only writes a nolimit.cmd shim that invokes node on the package's own.ad/x0.js entry (a workaround for npm's bin-shim handling of dotfile directories). No installer-side secrets are read at install or import time; no remote code is fetched or eval'd; the offensive functionality only fires when the operator explicitly invokes the CLI against their own targets. The harm here is to third-party victims of an operator who chooses to run this tool, not to the developer who installs the package. Nevertheless, the combination of pervasive obfuscation, hidden license-server URL, and offensive bulk-mailer/account-takeover capabilities warrants human review before allowing into a build pipeline — operators may be exposed to abuse-policy and legal risk, and the obfuscation prevents end-to-end code review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
